Hello Coders!

Django Argon Theme, a popular open-source project, has been updated to fully cover the default Django Authentication links (registration included). The product is released under the MIT license on GitHub and the sources can be used in commercial projects or eLearning activities without any constraints. Thanks for reading!

✨ Product Highlights

The design credit for this modern Bootstrap 5 design goes to Creative-Tim, a well-known web agency, and open-source contributor.

  • ✅ Argon Design - free Bootstrap 5 Kit
  • ✅ Django.contrib.AUTH pages - fully covered
  • ✅ KIT is available also for common users
  • ✅ Bonus: Registration page
  • ✅ MIT License
  • ✅ Free support via Email & Discord

Argon Dashboard is built with over 70 individual components, giving you the freedom of choosing and combining.

This Dashboard is coming with pre-built examples, so the development process is seamless, and switching from our pages to the real website is very easy to be done. Every element has multiple states for colors, styles, hover, and focus, that you can easily access and use.

This Django theme can be installed and used by following the steps listed in the README file:

👉 Step #1 - Install theme via PIP

$ pip install django-admin-argon-dashboard

👉 Step #2 - Add admin_argon application to the INSTALLED_APPS section

 INSTALLED_APPS = ( ... 'admin_argon.apps.AdminArgonConfig', 'django.contrib.admin', )

👉 Step #3 - Update project routing

 urlpatterns = [ ... path('', include('admin_argon.urls')), ]

Once the above settings are saved, here are the available links provided by the theme:

原文:

Almost all tutorials shows how to initialize a node app with Node installed locally. Here is a way to create a node app in Docker without installing Node locally.

Initialize a node app in the current directory with Docker:

  • Replace npm init with whatever npm command is needed 
docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/$(basename $PWD)" -w "/$(basename $PWD)" -it node:current-alpine sh -c "npm init"

Info

  • -rm remove
  • -v volume
  • $PWD path to current directory
  • basename last element of file path
  • -w working directory
  • -it interactive docker terminal
  • -c context, without it sh will not work

Items.Title: 您不需要 100% 的覆盖率。 | You don’t need 100% coverage.
Items.Description:
在考虑去 100% 甜蜜之前……你至少有足够的测试吗？
获得 100% 的任何功能都很棒，请不要误会我的意思。 但是，如果您对大部分应用程序没有足够的测试，那么您不需要 100%。

首先覆盖所有快乐路径

您的应用程序至少应该做的是在您按预期使用时按预期工作。

这意味着首先涵盖您所有的快乐路径。

覆盖核心功能

你的核心特征，也是那些更关键的特征，必然会给你带来更多的痛苦。

对你来说很重要，你可能首先想要他们 100%。

在所有事情中，你最关键的地方应该有 100%。 覆盖主要错误路径

如果有人尝试输入错误的密码，或者如果您的 API 返回他们无权执行某事，会发生什么情况？

您可能会遇到许多预期的错误，您还应该涵盖这些路径。

有错误吗？ 复制测试！

对于错误，您是否已经被覆盖并不重要（如果您有错误，那么您可能没有被覆盖）。

遇到错误，在测试中复制它，再也不用担心它了。

新功能可以从 100%（或几乎）开始

如果您开始致力于测试您的应用程序，则取决于您在测试所有内容时所处的位置，新功能可以从已经 100% 或尽可能接近的位置开始是显而易见的。

争取 100%？

在达到 100% 之前，您可能甚至不需要那个 100%，您可能需要检查您已经拥有的那些测试是否实际上涵盖了您的覆盖率所说的内容。

您可以“轻松”覆盖 100%，但如果您即使在测试场地上也一直存在错误和其他问题，那么这些 100% 可能毫无价值，如果您正在测试的实际上是在测试您想要的，那么您应该改进。

超过 100%（甚至没有 100%）

到现在为止，您将拥有很高的百分比，甚至可能在百分之九十的范围内。

所以你有一个选择……因为将最后一个百分比爬到 100% 代价高昂而且可能不值得，你可以考虑添加冗余或进行其他类型的测试。

您可能会有很多单元和集成测试，所以现在您可以考虑端到端测试，可能是负载和其他类型的压力测试，或者一些突变测试怎么样？

Cover Photo by Clay Banks on Unsplash Clay Banks在Unsplash上的封面照片



原文:

Before thinking about going for the sweet 100%… do you, at least, have enough tests?
Getting 100% for any feature you have is awesome, don’t get me wrong. But if you don’t have enough tests for most of your application, then you don’t need that 100%.

Cover all the happy paths first

The least your application should do is work as you intended when you use it as intended.

This means covering all your happy paths first.

Cover the core features

Your core features, also those more critical, are bound to give you more grief.

Being important to you, you probably want 100% for them first.

Out of everything, your most critical places should have 100%. (And probably some more, because of possible problems you can have and edge cases that will give you redundancy.)

Cover the main error paths

What happens when someone tries a wrong password or if your API returns that they aren’t authorized to do something?

There are many expected errors you can have and you should also cover those paths.

Got a bug? Replicate on tests!

For bugs, it doesn’t matter if you’re already covered (and if you got a bug, then you probably aren’t covered).

Got a bug, replicate it in a test and never bother about it again.

New features can start at 100% (or almost it)

Depending on where you are in testing everything if you started to commit yourself to testing your application, it’s a no-brainer that new features can start with already 100%, or as close as possible.

Go for 100%?

Before getting to 100%, and you probably don’t even need that 100%, you probably will need to check if those tests you already have are actually covering what your coverage says it does.

You can “easily” cover 100%, but if you keep having bugs and other problems even on tested grounds, then those 100% might not be worth anything and you should improve if what you’re testing is actually testing what you want.

Beyond 100% (even without 100%)

By now, you would have a high percentage, maybe even in the ninety percent range.

So you have a choice… because climbing the last percent to 100% is costly and might not be worth it, you can think about adding redundancy or doing other types of testing.

You probably would have lots of units and integration testing, so now you could think about e2e tests, maybe load and other types of stress testing, or how about some mutation tests?

Cover Photo by Clay Banks on Unsplash
Items.Author: noriller

Items.Title: 树立作为鼓舞人心的开发者的信心 | BUILDING CONFIDENCE AS AN INSPIRING DEVELOPER
Items.Description:
对于生活在当今社会的每个人来说，自信是格外重要和无可置疑的要求。 您应对困难、解决问题和与人打交道的能力需要自信。

幸运的是，自信是一种技能，例如驾驶汽车或打网球或篮球，可以通过实践来学习和发展。

首先，什么是信心？

自信对人来说可能意味着不同的东西，但在本文中，我将以 4 种方式定义自信。

  1. 信心是对未来和你所走的道路充满希望。
  2. 自信是知道你可以控制那条道路。
  3. 信心是对这些信息保持冷静，以及对当前情况的掌控感。
  4. 自信是这样一个事实，即了解你的洞察力，你的能力可以承受手头的任何任务。

我们有两种自信

  1. 对自己的信心，即您向世界投射的能量。 即你的肢体语言。
  2. 执行任务的信心。

我们将重点关注第二种信心； 现在的问题是，我们如何建立这种信心？

信心来自实践； 作为一名进入软件开发的新手； 你可能会因为领域太广而感到自卑和不知所措，当你听到高级开发人员的多年经验时，你也可能开始自卑； 所有这些感觉都是正常的，它发生在每个人身上。 但有趣的是； 你可以像他们一样。 但是怎么办？

  1. 向下定位：软件工程范围很广，所以你必须向下定位并一次专注于一个领域。 我们在该领域有不同的利基，例如前端工程、后端工程、全栈开发、DevOps 工程、安全工程等

  2. 从基础开始：在中学，您不能跳过常规的 1、2、3……。 或微积分指数。 这告诉你什么？ 您必须从第 1 阶段开始； 这同样适用于软件工程。 对于前端，在迁移到任何框架之前学习基本的 HTML、CSS 和 JS，对于后端，学习普通（没有框架）python 或 JS 用于 DevOps 脚本、云等的基本知识，学习基础知识和以它为基础会让你对即将到来的任何新框架或现有框架充满信心。

  3. 每天练习； 根据伍迪艾伦的说法，他说：

出现已经是成功的一半。

每天至少花 30 分钟编码。 养成每天编码的习惯，这将有助于建立你的大脑记忆。

让编码成为一种一致的习惯。 世上不需要喜怒无常的天才。 这与智力无关，而与一致性有关，而一致性可以积累经验和能力。

你编码的越多，你建立的信心就越大，你建立的信心越大，你在编程方面就越成功。

原文:

Confidence is exceptionally imperative and an unquestionable requirement for everybody living in today’s society. Your capacity to manage hardships, tackle issues, and approach people requires confidence.

Fortunately, confidence is a skill, such as driving a vehicle or playing tennis or basketball, that can be learned and developed with practice.

First, what is confidence?

Confidence can mean different things to people, but in this article, I will define confidence in 4 ways.

  1. Confidence is hopeful about the future and the path you are on.
  2. Confidence is knowing that you have control over that path.
  3. Confidence is being calm with that information and the feeling of command over your current circumstances.
  4. Confidence is the fact that knowing your insight, your capacity can endure any task that is at hand.

We have two types of confidence

  1. The confidence in yourself i.e the energy you project out to the world. i.e your body language.
  2. The confidence in performing a task.

We will focus on the second type of confidence; “The confidence in performing a task”

The question now is, how do we build this confidence?

Confidence comes from practice; As a newbie entering into software development; you might feel inferior and overwhelmed because the field Is too broad, and you might also begin to have low self-esteem when you hear senior developers’ numbers of years of experience; all these feelings are normal and it happens to everyone. But the interesting part is; you can be like Them. BUT HOW?

  1. Niche down: software engineering is broad, so you have to niche down and focus on one at a time. we have different niches in the field e.g front-end engineering, back-end engineering, full stack development, DevOps engineering, security engineering e.t.c

  2. Start from the basics: In secondary school, you can’t jump your regular 1,2,3…. Or indices to calculus. What does that tell you? You must start from stage 1; the same applies to software engineering. For the front-end learn your basic HTML, CSS, and JS before you move to any framework, for the back-end learn your vanilla (without framework) python or JS for DevOps your basic knowledge of scripting, cloud e.t.c. learning the fundamentals and being grounded in it gives you confidence for any new framework that is to come or an existing framework.

  3. Practice every day; According to Woody Allen, he said:

Showing up is already half the battle.

Spend at least 30 mins coding every day. Make a habit of daily coding, this will help build your brain memory.

Make coding a consistent habit. The world has no use for a temperamental genius. It’s not about intelligence, it is about consistency and consistency builds up experience and competence.

The more you code, the more confidence you build, and the more confidence you build, the more successful you become at programming.

Mehreen Mallick Fiona - Nov 14

Items.Title: 在 Ruby on Rails - React 应用程序中使用 Web API 集成 Sendgrid | Integrate Sendgrid using Web API in Ruby on Rails - React application
Items.Description:
纳尔逊芝布加西亚

发表于对于初学者来说，有时在 Rails API 中集成邮件系统总是具有挑战性。 在这篇博客中，我将逐步解释如何将 Sendgrid 集成到 Ruby on Rails-React 应用程序中。 该博客对初学者友好且易于理解。 在 Rails 中设置 Sendgrid 邮件系统一直让 Rails 初学者感到害怕，因为他们认为这是一个复杂的过程。 只是想让您知道，在 Rails 应用程序中设置 Sendgrid 并不复杂。

在 Web 应用程序中设置 Sendgrid 电子邮件系统有两种方法，即使用 Sendgrid Web API 和 Sendgrid SMTP 中继。 在此博客中，我将讨论使用 Web API 设置 Sendgrid，这是最推荐的方法。 让我们开始旅程吧。

创建 Rails 应用程序

让我们从创建我们的 Rails 应用程序开始。 使用rails new sendgrid --api创建 API rails 应用程序。

使用命令cd sendgrid进入 Rails 应用程序的目录。 使用用户名和电子邮件作为属性生成用户资源。 两者都是字符串。 命令是rails g resource user username email 。
运行rails db:migrate以运行迁移。
使用命令rails s启动 rails 服务器。

完成后，我们应该已经创建了路由、控制器和模型。 让我们继续在用户控制器和用户模型中添加一些代码。

在用户模型中添加验证

让我们添加一些验证以确保输入字段不为空。

 class User < ApplicationRecord validates :username , presence: true validates :email , presence: true end

在用户控制器中添加控制器操作

我们完成了用户模型，现在让我们修改我们的user_controller文件以创建一个新用户并验证用户名或电子邮件是否为空。 前往控制器文件夹并编辑user_controller .rb 文件。 让我们首先创建我们的索引方法，我们将使用它来测试我们的 API 以查看我们是否正确存储了数据。 编辑 userController 类以这种方式查看

class UsersController < ApplicationController # Get all users def index users = User . all render json: users end end

让我们添加一个创建方法来将用户添加到数据库中

# Create a user def create @user = User . create! ( user_params ) render json: { body: @user , message: "User created successfully" }, status: :created end

我们需要创建两个私有方法，一个用于允许用户参数，另一个用于验证用户输入。

 # Private methods private def user_params params . permit ( :username , :email ) end # Invalid entity response def invalid_entry ( invalid ) render json: { errors: invalid . record . errors . full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity end

然后在类的顶部为无效的用户实体添加一个救援

rescue_from ActiveRecord :: RecordInvalid , with: :invalid_entry

我们的用户控制器类现在看起来应该与此类似

class UsersController < ApplicationController rescue_from ActiveRecord :: RecordInvalid , with: :invalid_entry # Get all users def index users = User . all render json: users end # Create a user def create @user = User . create! ( user_params ) render json: { body: @user , message: "User created successfully" }, status: :created end # Private methods private def user_params params . permit ( :username , :email ) end # Invalid entity response def invalid_entry ( invalid ) render json: { errors: invalid . record . errors . full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity end end

现在让我们的用户控制器看起来像那样。 我们将在设置 Sendgrid 时回来编辑它。 接下来，让我们通过取消注释 config 目录的 initializers 文件夹中的 cors.rb 中的代码来添加 CORS。 为了确保我们的 cors 配置正常工作，我们需要取消注释 gem 文件中的 cors gem 并运行bundle install以安装必要的依赖项。 重新启动轨道服务器。

使用 postman 测试 API

我们需要测试我们的 API，看看它们是否运行良好。 打开postman尝试使用post方法创建一个新用户，然后使用get方法获取所有创建的用户。

好的，我们的 API 端点运行良好。

现在，让我们的前端开始工作

创建 React 应用程序

下一步是创建一个带有用户简单用户注册页面的 React 应用程序。 让我们从创建我们的 React 应用程序开始。 使用命令npx create-react-app client创建一个名为 client 的 React 应用程序 使用命令cd client进入 react app 目录并运行npm start以运行 react 应用程序。

创建注册页面

在客户端 src 文件夹中创建一个 Signup.js 文件。 创建一个名为 Signup 的功能组件，并在其中添加以下代码。

 import React from ' react ' function Signup () { return ( < div style = {{ width : " 300px " , marginLeft : " auto " , marginRight : " auto " }} > < h2 > User Signup < /h2 > < form style = {{ display : " flex " , flexDirection : " column " }} > < label htmlFor = ' username ' > Username : < /label> < input type = { ' text ' } id = " username " name = ' username ' />< br /> < label htmlFor = ' email ' > Email : < /label> < input type = { ' text ' } id = " email " name = ' email ' />< br /> < button type = ' submit ' > Submit < /button > < /form > < /div > ) } export default Signup

使用以下代码编辑 App.js

 import ' ./App.css ' ; import Signup from ' ./Signup ' ; function App () { return ( < div className = " App " > < Signup /> < /div > ); } export default App ;

在 Signup.js 上添加功能，使表单能够使用 API 端点将数据发送到服务器。

 import React , { useState } from ' react ' function Signup () { const [ data , setData ] = useState ({ username : '' , email : '' , }) const [ errors , setErrors ] = useState ([]) const [ message , setMessage ] = useState ( '' ) const [ success , setSuccess ] = useState ( false ) function handleChange ( e ){ let name = e . target . name let value = e . target . value setData ({... data , [ name ]: value }) } // POST user data function handleSubmit ( e ){ e . preventDefault () fetch ( ' http://localhost:3000/users ' ,{ method : " POST " , headers : { " Content-Type " : " application/json " }, body : JSON . stringify ( data ) }). then ( res => { if ( res . ok ){ res . json (). then ( user => { setMessage ( user . message ) setSuccess ( true ) }) } else { res . json (). then ( err => setErrors ( err . errors )) } }) } return ( < div style = {{ width : " 300px " , marginLeft : " auto " , marginRight : " auto " }} > < h2 > User Signup < /h2 > { success ? < h3 style = {{ color : " green " }} > { message } < /h3> : < div > { errors . map ( err =>< p key = { err } style = {{ color : " red " }} > { err } < /p> ) } < /div> } < form style = {{ display : " flex " , flexDirection : " column " }} onSubmit = { handleSubmit } > < label htmlFor = ' username ' > Username : < /label> < input type = { ' text ' } id = " username " name = ' username ' onChange = { handleChange } /><br/ > < label htmlFor = ' email ' > Email : < /label> < input type = { ' text ' } id = " email " name = ' email ' onChange = { handleChange } /><br/ > < button type = ' submit ' > Submit < /button > < /form > < /div > ) } export default Signup

设置发送网格

首先，您需要使用 sendgrid 创建一个帐户。 在设置帐户时填写所需的所有详细信息。 等待您的帐户被验证。 注意：任何用户都可以创建帐户，即使您没有公司或组织。 一旦您的帐户通过验证并且您可以查看您的仪表板，在左侧菜单中，单击电子邮件 API 和集成指南。 您的仪表板将显示两个电子邮件设置选项。 使用 Web API 并单击选择以将其选中。
接下来，选择 Ruby 作为您的语言


输入您的 API 密钥的名称，然后单击创建 API 以创建密钥。 按照有关创建存储 API 密钥的环境变量的说明进行操作。 在 Rails 应用程序的根目录中创建环境变量。 echo "export SENDGRID_API_KEY='YOUR API KEY'" > sendgrid.env
将文件添加到 git ignore，这样它就不会发送到你的 github 帐户。 这样做是为了避免暴露您的 API 密钥，一旦暴露，可能会导致您的 Sendgrid 帐户被暂停。 echo "sendgrid.env" >> .gitignore
设置源source ./sendgrid.env 。 我们稍后会将此 API 密钥移动到 credentials.yml.enc 以确保其安全使用。
接下来，将 sendgrid gem、 gem 'sendgrid-ruby'添加到 gem 文件并运行bundler或bundle install以安装依赖项。 重新启动轨道服务器。 将发送您的第一封电子邮件下给出的代码复制到应用程序中的 ruby​​ 文件中。 用您的详细信息替换给定的示例详细信息，然后尝试使用命令ruby filename.rb在终端上单独运行该文件。 您将收到一封发送到“收件人”电子邮件的电子邮件。 下一步只是将代码移动到一个类中，并在必要时重用该类。

将 API 密钥添加到 credentials.yml.enc 文件中

我们已经走了很长一段路，我们快到了，紧紧抓住，不要松手。 要编辑该文件，请在终端EDITOR="code --wait" rails credentials:edit上键入以下命令。 代码基本上代表您正在使用的代码编辑器。 我正在使用 VS 代码，因此将编辑器设置为代码。 您可以在rails Custom Credentials或stackoverflow上阅读有关编辑此文件的更多信息。 当文件在编辑模式下打开您的代码编辑时，添加 sendgrid API 密钥，如下所示： sendgrid: YOUR API KEY 。 保存并退出文件以保存更改。

创建 email_service.rb 文件

我将在应用程序文件夹的服务文件夹中创建电子邮件文件。 创建服务文件夹并添加 email_service.rb 文件。 创建一个与文件名匹配的类，因为这是 ruby​​ 中的约定。 在类中，创建一个名为class的类方法，并传入参数； 从、到、主题和内容。 然后从 sendgrid 中复制代码并编辑如下。 你的班级应该是这样的。

 require 'sendgrid-ruby' include SendGrid class EmailService def self . call from :, to :, subject :, content: from = Email . new ( email: from ) to = Email . new ( email: to ) subject = subject content = Content . new ( type: 'text/plain' , value: content ) mail = SendGrid :: Mail . new ( from , subject , to , content ) sg = SendGrid :: API . new ( api_key: Rails . application . credentials . sendgrid ) response = sg . client . mail . _ ( 'send' ). post ( request_body: mail . to_json ) puts response . status_code puts response . body puts response . headers puts Rails . application . credentials . sendgrid end end

请注意代码中的以下更改

  • 此代码已更改为获取在 credentials.yml.enc 文件中设置的 API 密钥sg = SendGrid::API.new(api_key: Rails.application.credentials.sendgrid)
  • 来自 sendgrid 的代码看起来像这样mail = Mail.new(from, subject, to, content) 。 自 sendgrid 的代码以来，我进行了更改，当我将其移至该类时，它显示有关缺少参数的错误。 将代码更改为上面的代码解决了问题。 编辑 user_controller 以在用户注册时发送邮件最后一步，通过添加电子邮件服务来编辑用户控制器。 我们正在使用 EmailService 类来发送电子邮件。 我们在类上调用EmailService的call类方法，传入参数。 编辑 create 方法中的代码，如下所示。
 def create @user = User . create! ( user_params ) @from = "useyouremail@gmail.com" @subject = "New User Account" @content = "Thank you for registering with us #{ @user . username } . Your account has been created successfully" EmailService . call ( from: @from , to: @user . email , subject: @subject , content: @content ) render json: { body: @user , message: "User created successfully" }, status: :created end

您的 UserController 类应该如下所示

class UsersController < ApplicationController rescue_from ActiveRecord :: RecordInvalid , with: :invalid_entry # Get all users def index users = User . all render json: users end # Create a user def create @user = User . create! ( user_params ) @from = "useyouremail@gmail.com" @subject = "New User Account" @content = "Thank you for registering with us #{ @user . username } . Your account has been created successfully" EmailService . call ( from: @from , to: @user . email , subject: @subject , content: @content ) render json: { body: @user , message: "User created successfully" }, status: :created end # Private methods private def user_params params . permit ( :username , :email ) end # Invalid entity response def invalid_entry ( invalid ) render json: { errors: invalid . record . errors . full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity end end

尝试使用有效的电子邮件注册用户，如果终端上打印的状态为 200s，则检查 Rails 服务器，这意味着电子邮件已成功发送。

原文:

Nelson Chibukasia

Posted on Nov 20

Sometimes it's always challenging to integrate a mailing system in Rails API for beginners. In this blog, I'll explain step by step on how to integrate Sendgrid in a Ruby on Rails-React application. The blog is beginner friendly and easy to understand. Setting up Sendgrid mailing system in Rails has always scared beginners in Rails as they think its a complex process. Just to let you know, there is nothing complex with setting up Sendgrid in Rails application.

There are two ways of setting up Sendgrid email system in a web application, that is using Sendgrid Web API and Sendgrid SMTP Relay. In this blog, I will discuss setting up Sendgrid using Web API which is the most recommended way. Let's begin the journey.

Create a Rails application

Lets begin by creating up our rails application. Use the rails new sendgrid --api to create a API rails application.

Get into the direcory of the rails application using the command cd sendgrid. Genrate a user resource with the username and email as the attributes. Both are strings. The command is rails g resource user username email.
. Run rails db:migrate to run the migrations.
. Start the rails server using the command rails s.

With that done, we shall have our routes, controller, and models already created. Let's go ahead and add some codes in the user controller and user model.

Add Validations in user model

Let's add some validations to make sure the input fields are not empty.

class User < ApplicationRecord validates :username, presence: true validates :email, presence: true end

Add controller actions in user controller

We are done with the user model, let's now modify our user_controller file to create a new user and validate if username or email is empty. Head to the controllers folder and edit the user_controller.rb file. Let's first create our index method that we shall use to test our API to see if we have our data in properly stored. Edit the userController class to look this this way

class UsersController < ApplicationController # Get all users  def index users= User.all render json: users end
end

Let's add a create method to add a user to the database

# Create a user  def create @user = User.create!(user_params) render json: {body: @user, message: "User created successfully"}, status: :created end

We need to create two private methods, one for permitting user parameters and the other for validating user input.

 # Private methods private def user_params params.permit(:username, :email) end # Invalid entity response  def invalid_entry(invalid) render json: {errors: invalid.record.errors.full_messages}, status: :unprocessable_entity end

Then add a rescue for the invalid user entity at the top of the class

rescue_from ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, with: :invalid_entry

Our user controller class should be looking similar to this at the moment

class UsersController < ApplicationController rescue_from ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, with: :invalid_entry # Get all users def index users= User.all render json: users end # Create a user  def create @user = User.create!(user_params) render json: {body: @user, message: "User created successfully"}, status: :created end # Private methods private def user_params params.permit(:username, :email) end # Invalid entity response  def invalid_entry(invalid) render json: {errors: invalid.record.errors.full_messages}, status: :unprocessable_entity end
end

Let's have our user controller looking that way at the moment. We shall come back to edit it when setting up Sendgrid. Next, let's add CORS by uncommenting the code in cors.rb in initializers folder in config directory. The code in cors.rb should look like this

Rails.application.config.middleware.insert_before 0, Rack::Cors do allow do origins "*" resource "*", headers: :any, methods: [:get, :post, :put, :patch, :delete, :options, :head] end
end

To make sure our cors configuration is working, we need to uncomment the cors gem in gem file and the run bundle install to install the necessary dependencies. Restart the rails server.

Test APIs using postman

We need to test our APIs and see if they are working perfectly. Open postman and try creating a new user using the post method, and then get all the users created using the get method.

Okay, our API endpoints are working fine.

Now, let's get our front-end working

Create React App

The next step is creating a react application with a user simple user signup page. Let's begin by creating our react application. Use the command npx create-react-app client to create a react app called client . Get in the react app directory using the command cd client and run npm start to run the react application.

Create a sign up page

Create a Signup.js file in the clients src folder. Create a functional component called Signup and add the following code in it.

import React from 'react' function Signup() { return ( <div style={{width: "300px", marginLeft: "auto", marginRight: "auto"}}> <h2>User Signup</h2>
 <form style={{display: "flex", flexDirection: "column"}}> <label htmlFor='username'>Username:</label>  <input type={'text'} id= "username" name='username' /><br/> <label htmlFor='email'>Email:</label>  <input type={'text'} id= "email" name='email' /><br/> <button type='submit'>Submit</button>
 </form>
 </div>
 )
} export default Signup

Edit the App.js with the following code

import './App.css';
import Signup from './Signup'; function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <Signup /> </div>
 );
} export default App;

Add the functionalities on Signup.js to enable the form to send data to the server using the API endpoint.

import React, { useState } from 'react' function Signup() { const [data, setData] = useState({ username: '', email: '', }) const [errors, setErrors] = useState([]) const [message, setMessage] = useState('') const [success, setSuccess] = useState(false) function handleChange(e){ let name = e.target.name let value = e.target.value setData({...data, [name]: value}) } // POST user data function handleSubmit(e){ e.preventDefault() fetch('http://localhost:3000/users',{ method: "POST", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify(data) }).then(res=>{ if(res.ok){ res.json().then(user=>{ setMessage(user.message) setSuccess(true) }) }else{ res.json().then(err=>setErrors(err.errors)) } }) } return ( <div style={{width: "300px", marginLeft: "auto", marginRight: "auto"}}> <h2>User Signup</h2>
 {success? <h3 style={{color: "green"}}>{message}</h3>:
 <div> {errors.map(err=><p key={err} style={{color: "red"}}>{err}</p>)}
 </div>}
 <form style={{display: "flex", flexDirection: "column"}} onSubmit={handleSubmit}> <label htmlFor='username'>Username:</label>  <input type={'text'} id= "username" name='username' onChange={handleChange} /><br/> <label htmlFor='email'>Email:</label>  <input type={'text'} id= "email" name='email' onChange={handleChange}/><br/> <button type='submit'>Submit</button>
 </form>
 </div>
 )
} export default Signup

Set up sendgrid

First, you need to create an account with sendgrid. Fill in all the details required while setting the account. Wait for your account to be verified. NOTE: Any user can create the account even if you don't have a company or organization. Once your account has been verified and you can view your dashboard, on the left menu, click on email API and the Integration guide. Your dashboard will display two options for email set up. Use Web API and click choose to select it.
.
Next, select Ruby as your language


Enter the name of your API key and click create API to create the key. Follow the instructions on creating the environment variable that shall store the API key. Create the environment variable in the root of your rails application. echo "export SENDGRID_API_KEY='YOUR API KEY'" > sendgrid.env
Add the file to the git ignore so that it is not sent to your github account. This is done to avoid exposing your API key which on exposure, may lead to suspension of your Sendgrid account. echo "sendgrid.env" >> .gitignore
Set the source source ./sendgrid.env. We will later move this API key to a credentials.yml.enc to keep it safe for use.
Next, add the sendgrid gem, gem 'sendgrid-ruby' to the gem file and run bundler or bundle install to install the dependencies. Restart the rails server. Copy the code given under send your first email into a ruby file in your application. Replace the sample details given with your details and then try and run the file separately on the terminal using the command, ruby filename.rb. You will get an email sent to the "to" email. The next step is just moving the code to a class and reusing the class where necessary.

Add the API key into the credentials.yml.enc file

We have come a long way and we are almost there, hang in tight, don't let go. The credentials.yml.enc file (Located in the config directory) is encrypted and therefore cannot be edited directly. To edit the file, type the following command on the terminal EDITOR="code --wait" rails credentials:edit. Code basically represents the code editor you are using. I'm using VS code thus setting the editor to code. You can read more about editing this file on rails Custom Credentials or stackoverflow. When the file opens on your code edit in the edit mode, add the sendgrid API key as follows: sendgrid: YOUR API KEY. The API key should not be in quotes. Save and exit from the file to save changes.

Create the email_service.rb file

I'll have the email file created in a services folder in the apps folder. Create services folder and add in an email_service.rb file. Create a class matching the file name as it is a convention in ruby. in the class, create a class method called class and pass in the parameters; from, to, subject, and content. Then copy the code from sendgrid and edit it as follows. Your class should look like this.

require 'sendgrid-ruby'
include SendGrid
class EmailService def self.call from:, to:, subject:, content: from = Email.new(email: from) to = Email.new(email: to) subject = subject content = Content.new(type: 'text/plain', value: content) mail = SendGrid::Mail.new(from, subject, to, content) sg = SendGrid::API.new(api_key: Rails.application.credentials.sendgrid) response = sg.client.mail._('send').post(request_body: mail.to_json) puts response.status_code puts response.body puts response.headers puts Rails.application.credentials.sendgrid end
end

Note the following changes from the code

  • This code has been changed to get the API key set in the credentials.yml.enc file sg = SendGrid::API.new(api_key: Rails.application.credentials.sendgrid)
  • The code on mail = SendGrid::Mail.new(from, subject, to, content) may slightly look different from the one provided on sendgrid account. The code from sendgrid looks like this mail = Mail.new(from, subject, to, content). I made the changes since the code from sendgrid, when I moved it to the class, it was showing an error about missing parameters. Changing the code to look as the one above solved the problem. Edit the user_controller to send mail on user signup The last step, edit the user controller by adding the email service. We are using the EmailService class to send the email. We call the call class method of EmailService on the class and pass in the arguments. Edit the code in the create method to look as follows. 

def create @user = User.create!(user_params) @from = "useyouremail@gmail.com" @subject = "New User Account" @content = "Thank you for registering with us #{@user.username}. Your account has been created successfully" EmailService.call(from: @from, to: @user.email, subject: @subject, content: @content) render json: {body: @user, message: "User created successfully"}, status: :created end

Your UserController class should look like the one below

class UsersController < ApplicationController rescue_from ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, with: :invalid_entry # Get all users def index users= User.all render json: users end # Create a user  def create @user = User.create!(user_params) @from = "useyouremail@gmail.com" @subject = "New User Account" @content = "Thank you for registering with us #{@user.username}. Your account has been created successfully" EmailService.call(from: @from, to: @user.email, subject: @subject, content: @content) render json: {body: @user, message: "User created successfully"}, status: :created end # Private methods private def user_params params.permit(:username, :email) end # Invalid entity response  def invalid_entry(invalid) render json: {errors: invalid.record.errors.full_messages}, status: :unprocessable_entity end
end

Try and register a user with a valid email and check the rails server if the status printed on the terminal is 200s, which means an email has been sent successfully.

User Signup


Signup email sent


Github repo link That's it, bye and Happy Coding!

Items.Title: 我是如何升级我的 Fedora 安装的，或者“你不需要“X things to do”列表” | How I've upgraded my Fedora installation, or 'you don't need "X things to do" lists'
Items.Description:
您可能已经看过很多“安装 Fedora 后要做的 10 件事”教程。 这不是教程。 您不会找到关于更新操作系统、破坏性“性能”调整或要安装的 shell 扩展列表的明显提醒。

我们不使用 Windows，无需删除或更改默认内容即可使操作系统可用。

让我们通过几个简单的步骤完成更新过程（Fedora 36 到 37，使用 GNOME），并在此过程中学习一两件事。

重新安装操作系统而不是就地升级

百胜到DNF。 如果您有单独的/home分区（和备份！），您可以在保留所有用户数据和大多数用户设置的同时重新安装操作系统。

使用“自定义”分区方法，解锁任何加密的系统磁盘，并重新使用现有分区。 只需确保禁用/home （您的文件）和/boot/efi （其他固件文件）的“重新格式化”复选框。 稍后出现提示时使用您的旧用户名。

图 1. 升级成功。 安装的应用程序仍然固定在 Dock 上，并且以前的字体设置得到尊重，即使某些字体丢失了。

重新启用硬件加速

不幸的是，这意味着您不能拥有默认提供的专有驱动程序和多媒体编解码器，即使您生活在软件专利不存在的国家也是如此。

您可能听说过最近因更新软件图形库 Mesa而导致的限制。

这就是为什么大多数教程建议您添加RPM Fusion的原因，这是一个额外的软件存储库。 当您想在线观看视频时它可能很有用，但启用 RPM Fusion 还不够。 您需要在 Firefox 上安装用于硬件加速的精选软件包，这在Fedora wiki中有所描述 - 除非绝对必要，否则无需进行可疑的about:config更改。

图 2. vainfo命令的输出：为第 11 代英特尔 Xe 显卡安装额外包之前和之后。

重新启用 Flatpak 存储库

如果您在 Fedora 存储库或 RPM Fusion 中找不到某些软件（例如心爱的 Extension Manager），您的下一个选择可能是 Flathub。 您可以一键启用它。 然后可以从命令行或软件应用程序安装应用程序（可能需要重新启动）。

还有……就这些？

是的。 不到一小时，您应该安装了干净的操作系统，其中包含您在主分区上收集的所有垃圾。 随时下载丢失的应用程序，并在您离开的地方继续您的工作。

您不需要遵循“之后要做的 X 件事”列表。 遵循你的常识。



原文:

You may have already seen a lot of "10 things to do after installing Fedora" tutorials. This is not a tutorial. You won't find obvious reminders about updating the operating system, destructive "performance" tweaks, or a list of shell extensions to be installed.

We are not using Windows, there's no need to remove or change default stuff to make an operating system usable.

Let's go through the update process (Fedora 36 to 37, with GNOME) in a few simple steps, and learn a thing or two along the way.

Reinstall OS instead of in-place upgrades

Yum to DNF. Xorg to Wayland. PulseAudio to PipeWire. Fedora is a moving target, and you don't want to keep old configuration files and compatibility workarounds around. If you have separate /home partition (and backups!), you can reinstall the OS while keeping all user data and most user settings.

Use "Custom" partitioning method, unlock any encrypted system disks, and re-use existing partitions. Just make sure "Reformat" checkbox is disabled for /home (your files) and /boot/efi (additional firmware files). Use your old username when prompted later on.

Fig 1. Successful upgrade. Installed apps are still pinned to the Dock, and previous font settings are respected, even if some fonts are missing.

Re-enable hardware acceleration

Fedora is sponsored by Red Hat, which means it has to comply to American law, including software patents. Unfortunately, this means you can't have proprietary drivers and multimedia codecs offered by default, even if you live in the country where software patents are not a thing.

You may have heard about recent restrictions caused by updates to Mesa, software graphics library.

This is why most tutorials recommend you to add RPM Fusion, the additional software repository. It might be useful when you want to watch videos online, but having RPM Fusion enabled is not enough. You need to install select packages for hardware acceleration on Firefox, which is described in Fedora wiki - no need for dubious about:config changes unless absolutely necessary.

Fig 2. Output of vainfo command: before and after installing extra packages for 11th gen Intel Xe Graphics.

Re-enable Flatpak repositories

If you can't find some software in Fedora repositories or in RPM Fusion (for example the beloved Extension Manager), your next choice is probably Flathub. You can enable it with one click. Apps can be then installed either from command line or from Software app (it may require a restart).

And... that's it?

Yes. Less than one hour in, you should have clean OS installation with all the junk you collected on your home partition. Download missing apps as you go, and continue your work where you left it.

You don't need to follow "X things to do after" lists. Follow your common sense.
Items.Author: jmpbne

Items.Title: OSD600 - Telescope - 测试提要 URL | OSD600 - Telescope - Testing for feed URLs
Items.Description:
上周，我处理了塞内卡望远镜项目的一个问题。 注册过程中有一个部分必须输入博客 URL。 然后使用它来获取提要 URL 列表，这些 URL 用于获取用户的帖子。 这需要更改，以便可以提供提要 URL，然后它会简单地显示在提要 URL 列表中。

我查看了用于从博客 URL 获取提要 URL 的服务，并注意到它采用博客 URL 的 html 响应并通过对照有效提要列表检查type属性值来获取链接（ <link>标签）值。 因此，我决定使用类似的方法，获取提供的 URL 的 html 响应，并根据提要的有效 MIME 类型列表检查Content-Type标头。 我最终更新了逻辑以测试 URL 是否为提要 URL，如果为真则返回它。 如果发现 URL 不是提要 URL，它会假设该 URL 是博客 URL 来尝试获取提要 URL。
我测试并确认新逻辑对博客和提要 URL 都有效。 然后，我为我添加的用于测试提要 URL 的新功能添加了一些测试。 测试最终比我预期的要简单，因为我所要做的就是模拟测试 url 的响应（使用nock ），然后检查该函数是否为 url 返回了正确的布尔值。
我创建了一个PR并注意到另一个文件中的一些测试现在失败了。 在调查此问题时，我收到了对我的 PR 的审查，要求我向测试失败的文件添加另一个测试。 该文件测试了整个 API 服务。 我发现 nock 默认情况下只模拟一个 URL 对一个请求的响应。 因为我现在也在检查提要 URL，所以从博客 URL 返回提要 URL 的函数抛出了一个错误，因为它的 nock 用完了。 为了解决这个问题，我必须在 nock 语句中指定模拟两个请求的 URL 响应：

 nock ( blogUrl ). get ( ' / ' ). twice (). reply ( 200 , mockBlogUrlResponseBody , { ' Content-Type ' : ' text/html ' , });

我修复了失败的测试并添加了一个新测试来检查尝试从提要 URL 获取提要 URL 是否会从 API 服务返回提要 URL。 最初我是这样设置的：

 [ ' application/xml ' , ' application/rss+xml ' , ' application/atom+xml ' , ' application/x.atom+xml ' , ' application/x-atom+xml ' , ' application/json ' , ' application/json+oembed ' , ' application/xml+oembed ' , ]. forEach ( async ( type ) => { nock (...) const res = await request ( app ) . post ( ' / ' ) . set ( ' Authorization ' , `bearer ${ createServiceToken ()} ` ) . send ([ feedUrl ]); expect ( res . status ). toBe ( 200 ); ...

然而，虽然使用jest运行测试会导致通过，但预期实际上会失败，并且失败结果会在测试结束后显示。 我很困惑为什么会发生一段时间。 最终，我在文件中注意到了这一点：

 afterEach (() => { nock . cleanAll (); });

据我了解，测试套件会运行，箭尾会在请求完成之前得到清理，然后是预期。 因此，预期会在测试套件完成后失败。 为了解决这个问题，我不得不寻找另一种方法来对一组数据运行相同的测试。 我最终使用了

it . concurrent . each ([ ' application/xml ' , ' application/rss+xml ' , ' application/atom+xml ' , ' application/x.atom+xml ' , ' application/x-atom+xml ' , ' application/json ' , ' application/json+oembed ' , ' application/xml+oembed ' , ])( ' ... %s ' , async ( type ) => { ...( ' original test from inside the loop ' )

这确保了测试在测试运行停止之前完成运行。 它还允许我对不同的测试值使用单个测试。 也可以使用“... %s”中的 %s 基于数组构建测试语句。

审查的另一件事是允许提要 URL 可能会破坏后端，因为它现在会将提要 URL 存储为博客 URL。 因此，我对此进行了调查，并感谢来自alexsam29的一些有用信息，我发现博客 URL 仅存储在提要模型的 html url 字段中。 此外，我没有找到任何使用该字段的地方。 在telescope 上显示的帖子中，还有一个指向博客的链接（不是提要，而是博客页面）。 但是，此链接不会根据博客 URL 呈现。 相反，博客提要包含一个<link>元素，该元素具有博客 URL，用于将帖子链接到博客：

 <rss xmlns:atom="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom" xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/" version="2.0"> <channel> <title>DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻: Eakam</title> <description>The latest articles on DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 by Eakam (@eakam).</description> <link>https://dev.to/eakam</link>

因此，我得出结论，此更改不会破坏功能。 我通过注册两个用户在本地对此进行了测试——一个使用博客 URL，另一个使用提要 URL，但没有发现任何区别。 所以，我在 PR 中添加了关于我的评论。



原文:

Last week, I worked on an issue for the Seneca telescope project. The sign-up process has a section where a blog URL must be entered. This is then used to get a list of feed URLs which are used to get the posts for a user. This needed to be changed so that a feed URL could be given instead, which would then simply get shown in the feed URL list.

I looked at the service which is used to get the feed URLs from a blog URL and noticed it takes the html response of the blog URL and gets the links (<link> tags) by checking the type attribute value against a list of valid feed values. So, I decided to use a similar approach by getting the html response for a provided URL and checking the Content-Type header against a list of valid MIME types for a feed. I ended up updating the logic to test if a URL is a feed URL, returning it if true. If the URL is found to not be a feed URL, it would try to get the feed URLs assuming the URL is a blog URL.
I tested and confirmed that the new logic worked for both blog and feed URLs. Then, I added some tests for the new function I added to test for a feed URL. Testing this ended up being simpler than I expected as all I had to do was mock the response of a test url (using nock), and then check if the function returned the correct boolean value for a url.
I created a PR and noticed that some of the tests in another file were now failing. While I was investigating this, I got a review on my PR, requesting me to add another test to the file which had the failing tests. That file tested the API service as a whole. I found out that nock only mocks a URL's response for one request by default. And since I was now checking for a feed URL as well, the function which returned the feed URLs from a blog URL was throwing an error since the nock for that was used up. To fix this, I had to specify in the nock statement to mock the URL response for two requests:

nock(blogUrl).get('/').twice().reply(200, mockBlogUrlResponseBody, { 'Content-Type': 'text/html',
});

I fixed the failing tests and added a new test to check if trying to get the feed URLs from a feed URLs would return the feed URL back from the API service. Initially I set it up like this:

 [ 'application/xml', 'application/rss+xml', 'application/atom+xml', 'application/x.atom+xml', 'application/x-atom+xml', 'application/json', 'application/json+oembed', 'application/xml+oembed', ].forEach(async (type) => { nock(...) const res = await request(app) .post('/') .set('Authorization', `bearer ${createServiceToken()}`) .send([feedUrl]); expect(res.status).toBe(200); ...

However, while running the tests using jest would result in a pass, the expectations would actually fail and the failure result would show up after the tests have already ended. I was confused as to why was happening for a while. Eventually, I noticed this in the file:

afterEach(() => { nock.cleanAll();
});

From what I understood, the test suite would run, the nocks would get cleaned before the request finished, which would be followed by the expectations. Thus, the expectations would fail after the test suite had finished. To fix this, I had to look up another way to run the same test for an array of data. I ended up using the

it.concurrent.each([ 'application/xml', 'application/rss+xml', 'application/atom+xml', 'application/x.atom+xml', 'application/x-atom+xml', 'application/json', 'application/json+oembed', 'application/xml+oembed',
])('... %s', async (type) => {
...('original test from inside the loop')

This ensured that the tests finished running before the test run would stop. It also allowed me to use a single test for different test values. The test statement can also be build based on the array using the %s in '... %s'.

Another thing from the review was that allowing a feed URL could break the back end as it would now store a feed URL as a blog URL. So, I investigated this and thanks to some helpful information from alexsam29, I found that the blog URL was only stored in a html url field of the feed model. Further, I did not find any places where that field was being used. In the posts that are shown on telescope, there is also a link to the blog (not the feed but the blog page). However, this link is not rendered based on the blog URL. Instead, a blog feed includes a <link> element which has the blog URL, and that is used to link a post to a blog:

<rss xmlns:atom="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom" xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/" version="2.0">
<channel>
<title>DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻: Eakam</title>
<description>The latest articles on DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 by Eakam (@eakam).</description>
<link>https://dev.to/eakam</link>

Therefore, I concluded that this change would not break and functionality. I tested this locally by registering two users- one with a blog URL, and one with a feed URL, and did not notice any difference. So, I added a comment about my this to the PR.

Items.Title: 在 Ubuntu 上使用 brew 安装 OpenCV for Java | Install OpenCV for Java using brew on Ubuntu
Items.Description:
阿米特·米斯拉

发表于在周末学习新东西是我们所有人都会做的事情，这个周末，我开始探索 OpenCV。 接下来是我在 Ubnutu 20.04 上使用 OpenCV 4.6.0 设置系统的演练。

概述

安装过程并不是那么简单，特别是如果您想使用 Java 作为您选择的编程语言。 周围有一些文档，但我最终采用了一条不同的路线，它像魅力一样工作，没有任何重大问题。

在 ubuntu 上设置brew

这一步很简单，您需要做的就是按照他们网站上提到的步骤进行操作 -
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

安装完成后，脚本将要求您在终端中执行以下命令。 请注意，这些命令不是可选的。

 $ echo '# Set PATH, MANPATH, etc., for Homebrew.' >> $HOME/.zprofile $ echo 'eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)"' >> $HOME/.zprofile $ eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)" ## Additional commands $ sudo apt-get install build-essential $ brew install gcc

此外，如果您像我一样使用zsh ，请将以下行添加到您的$HOME/.zshrc /.zshrc 文件中 - source $HOME/.zprofile

使用brew安装openCV

  • 让我们开始像这样使用brew安装ant - brew install ant 。 如果您希望最后的安装步骤生成opencv-java460.jar文件和关联的共享库，则这是强制性的。
  • 接下来执行以下命令并将标志-DBUILD_opencv_java更新为ON （来源）
  • 您可以通过执行以下命令brew doctor检查您的系统是否准备好安装opencv 。 输出应该看起来像这样，这是可以理解的，因为我们在最后一步中更改了标志
$ brew doctor Please note that these warnings are just used to help the Homebrew maintainers with debugging if you file an issue. If everything you use Homebrew for is working fine: please don't worry or file an issue; just ignore this. Thanks! Warning: You have uncommitted modifications to Homebrew/homebrew-core. If this is a surprise to you, then you should stash these modifications. Stashing returns Homebrew to a pristine state but can be undone should you later need to do so for some reason. cd /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/Homebrew/Library/Taps/homebrew/homebrew-core && git stash && git clean -d -f Uncommitted files: M Formula/opencv.rb
  • 最后执行命令在您的系统上安装openCV ，就像这样 - brew install opencv --build-from-source

检查安装

安装命令完成后，您应该一切顺利。 检查命令brew info opencv的输出，它应该看起来像这样 -

 $ brew info opencv ==> opencv: stable 4.6.0 (bottled) Open source computer vision library https://opencv.org/ /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/Cellar/opencv/4.6.0_1 (865 files, 324MB) * Built from source on 2022-11-20 at 18:33:21 From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/opencv.rb License: Apache-2.0 ==> Dependencies Build: cmake ✔, pkg-config ✔ Required: ceres-solver ✔, eigen ✔, ffmpeg ✔, glog ✔, harfbuzz ✔, jpeg-turbo ✔, libpng ✔, libtiff ✔, numpy ✔, openblas ✔, openexr ✔, openjpeg ✔, protobuf ✔, python@3.10 ✔, tbb ✔, vtk ✔, webp ✔, zlib ✔ ==> Analytics install: 16,116 (30 days), 47,026 (90 days), 194,148 (365 days) install-on-request: 15,595 (30 days), 45,590 (90 days), 187,137 (365 days) build-error: 96 (30 days)

确认您在安装文件夹下创建了必要的共享库和 jar 文件，如下所示 -

 $ ls /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/Cellar/opencv/4.6.0_1/share/java/opencv4 libopencv_java460.so opencv-460.jar

为maven准备opencv-java460.jar

获得最终的opencv_java460.jar ，您可以将其安装到本地存储库以用于开发目的。 这确保您不会制作 jar 文件的多个副本或引用具有linuxbrew主目录异常路径的外部 jar。 我使用了以下命令 -

 mvn org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-install-plugin:3.1.0:install-file -Dfile=/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/Cellar/opencv/4.6.0_1/share/java/opencv4/opencv-460.jar \ -DgroupId=org.opencv \ -DartifactId=opencv-java \ -Dversion=4.6.0_1 \ -Dpackaging=jar \ -DlocalRepositoryPath=$HOME/.m2

上面命令的输出应该是这样的 -

 ... ... [INFO] pom.xml not found in opencv-460.jar [INFO] Installing /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/Cellar/opencv/4.6.0_1/share/java/opencv4/opencv-460.jar to $HOME/.m2/org/opencv/opencv-java/4.6.0_1/opencv-java-4.6.0_1.jar [INFO] Installing /tmp/mvninstall7616729450445116135.pom to $HOME/.m2/org/opencv/opencv-java/4.6.0_1/opencv-java-4.6.0_1.pom [INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------ [INFO] BUILD SUCCESS [INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------ [INFO] Total time: 2.325 s [INFO] Finished at: 2022-11-20T18:41:42-05:00 [INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------

发布这个你可以像这样在你最喜欢的项目中添加opencv-java作为依赖项 -

 <dependency> <groupId>org.opencv</groupId> <artifactId>opencv-java</artifactId> <version>4.6.0_1</version> <dependency>

陷阱

在安装过程中，当我执行brew install opencv --build-from-source命令时，我遇到too many open files的问题。 为了解决这个问题，我必须像这样增加ulimit ulimit -n 2048 。

一旦暂停，amitmisra16 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，amitmisra16 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

原文:

Amit Misra

Posted on Nov 20

Learning new stuff over the weekend is something we all do and this weekend, I started exploring OpenCV. What follows next is my walkthrough for setting up my system with OpenCV 4.6.0 on Ubnutu 20.04.

Overview

The installation process wasn't that straightforward, specially if you want to use Java as your programming language of choice. There are a few documentation around but I ended up taking a different route which worked like charm without any major issues.

Setup brew on ubuntu

This step is trivial all you need to do is follow the steps mentioned on their website -
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

Once the installation completes the script will ask you to execute the following commands in the terminal. Please be advised, these commands are not optional.

$ echo '# Set PATH, MANPATH, etc., for Homebrew.' >> $HOME/.zprofile
$ echo 'eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)"' >> $HOME/.zprofile
$ eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)"

## Additional commands
$ sudo apt-get install build-essential
$ brew install gcc

Additionally if you are using zsh like I am, add the following line to your $HOME/.zshrc file - source $HOME/.zprofile

Install openCV using brew

  • Let's start by installing ant using brew like so - brew install ant. This is mandatory in case you want the final installation step to generate the opencv-java460.jar file and the associated shared library.
  • Next execute the following command and update the flag -DBUILD_opencv_java to be ON (source)
  • You can check if your system is ready for opencv to be installed by executing the following command - brew doctor. The output should look something like so, which is understandable as we changed the flag in the last step 
$ brew doctor       
Please note that these warnings are just used to help the Homebrew maintainers
with debugging if you file an issue. If everything you use Homebrew for is
working fine: please don't worry or file an issue; just ignore this. Thanks!

Warning: You have uncommitted modifications to Homebrew/homebrew-core.
If this is a surprise to you, then you should stash these modifications.
Stashing returns Homebrew to a pristine state but can be undone
should you later need to do so for some reason.
  cd /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/Homebrew/Library/Taps/homebrew/homebrew-core && git stash && git clean -d -f

Uncommitted files:
   M Formula/opencv.rb
  • Finally execute the command to install openCV on your system like so - brew install opencv --build-from-source

Checking the installation

Once the install command finishes, you should be all good. Check the output of the command brew info opencv it should look something like so -

$ brew info opencv                       
==> opencv: stable 4.6.0 (bottled)
Open source computer vision library
https://opencv.org/
/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/Cellar/opencv/4.6.0_1 (865 files, 324MB) *
  Built from source on 2022-11-20 at 18:33:21
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/opencv.rb
License: Apache-2.0
==> Dependencies
Build: cmake ✔, pkg-config ✔
Required: ceres-solver ✔, eigen ✔, ffmpeg ✔, glog ✔, harfbuzz ✔, jpeg-turbo ✔, libpng ✔, libtiff ✔, numpy ✔, openblas ✔, openexr ✔, openjpeg ✔, protobuf ✔, python@3.10 ✔, tbb ✔, vtk ✔, webp ✔, zlib ✔
==> Analytics
install: 16,116 (30 days), 47,026 (90 days), 194,148 (365 days)
install-on-request: 15,595 (30 days), 45,590 (90 days), 187,137 (365 days)
build-error: 96 (30 days)

Confirm you have the necessary shared library and jar file created under the installation folder like so -

$ ls /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/Cellar/opencv/4.6.0_1/share/java/opencv4 
libopencv_java460.so  opencv-460.jar

Prep opencv-java460.jar for maven

Once you have the final opencv_java460.jar, you can install it to your local repository for development purposes. This ensures you are not making multiple copies of the jar file or referring to an external jar with unusual path for linuxbrew home directory. I used the following command for the same -

mvn org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-install-plugin:3.1.0:install-file  -Dfile=/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/Cellar/opencv/4.6.0_1/share/java/opencv4/opencv-460.jar \
-DgroupId=org.opencv \
-DartifactId=opencv-java \
-Dversion=4.6.0_1 \
-Dpackaging=jar \
-DlocalRepositoryPath=$HOME/.m2

The output of the above command should something like so -

...
...
[INFO] pom.xml not found in opencv-460.jar
[INFO] Installing /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/Cellar/opencv/4.6.0_1/share/java/opencv4/opencv-460.jar to $HOME/.m2/org/opencv/opencv-java/4.6.0_1/opencv-java-4.6.0_1.jar
[INFO] Installing /tmp/mvninstall7616729450445116135.pom to $HOME/.m2/org/opencv/opencv-java/4.6.0_1/opencv-java-4.6.0_1.pom
[INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------
[INFO] BUILD SUCCESS
[INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------
[INFO] Total time:  2.325 s
[INFO] Finished at: 2022-11-20T18:41:42-05:00
[INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------

Post this you can add opencv-java as a dependency in your favorite project like so -

<dependency>
    <groupId>org.opencv</groupId>
    <artifactId>opencv-java</artifactId>
    <version>4.6.0_1</version>
<dependency>

Gotchas

During the installation I ran into an issue with too many open files when I executed the brew install opencv --build-from-source command. In order to resolve that I have to increase the ulimit like so - ulimit -n 2048.

Items.Title: 如何在 Windows 10 上安装适用于 Linux 的 Windows 子系统 (WSL) | How to install Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) on Windows 10
Items.Description:
发表于当我在ALX赞助的Holberton School开始我的软件工程项目时，我们得到了一个沙箱来运行命令和程序，但沙箱有局限性。 对我来说最大的限制之一是不断需要互联网连接来访问和使用沙箱，连接中断意味着重新连接，这在当时是没有动力的，必须有另一种方式。 如果您最近加入了 ALX 软件工程计划，那么这是给您的。

让我们开始吧，WSL 是一个虚拟化平台，它允许 Linux 发行版（例如 Ubuntu）在 Windows 中运行。 您可以不受限制地在沙盒上做任何事情。

第 1 步 - 打开 Windows 功能

第一步是转到开始栏的左下角，然后单击 Windows 图标，在搜索栏中键入“打开/关闭 Windows 功能” 。 该选项将如下所示，单击它。

第 2 步 - 为 Linux 启用 Windows 子系统

滚动浏览选项，直到显示适用于 Linux 的 Windows 子系统。 选中该框并单击确定以保存更改。 将弹出一个新窗口，其中包含立即或稍后重新启动计算机的选项。 在继续之前，您需要立即重新启动计算机。

第 3 步 - 下载 Ubuntu

重新启动计算机后，您可以选择从Microsoft Store或从Ubuntu 下载 Ubuntu 。


上述任何选项都将在您的计算机上下载并提供 Ubuntu 发行版。

第 4 步 - 通过 Ubuntu 或命令提示符访问终端

转到开始栏并单击 Windows 图标，查看Ubuntu或Bash on Ubuntu的应用程序（就像我的情况一样，请参见下图）。
单击以启动，将弹出终端。 另一种选择是从 Windows 命令提示符访问终端。 转到开始栏的左下角，单击 Windows 图标，在搜索栏中键入“命令提示符” 。 单击该选项后，将弹​​出命令提示符。 输入bash并按回车键访问终端。

恭喜！ 您现在可以在计算机上运行 shell 命令，而无需连接互联网或依赖内联网提供的沙箱。 在最初几周使用沙箱可能会很舒服，但是随着项目的到来，它会变得有点不安，所以请听取你大哥的建议并在你的计算机上安装 WSL。

还有另一种安装Ubuntu (Vagrant)的方法，您将在内部网上遇到（如果您还没有遇到过），但本文的范围仅限于WSL 。 如果这篇文章有帮助，请在下面的评论框中发表评论。

原文:

Ajisafe Oluwapelumi

Posted on Nov 20

When I started my software engineering program at Holberton School sponsored by ALX, we were provided with a sandbox to run commands and programs but the sandbox had limitations. One of the biggest limitations for me was the constant need for internet connection to access and use the sandbox, a loss in connection would mean reconnecting and it was de-motivating at the time, there had to be another way. If you joined the ALX Software Engineering Program recently then this is for you.

Let's get into it, WSL is a virtualisation platform that allows Linux distributions (e.g. Ubuntu) to run within windows. You get to do everything you do on the sandbox without the limitations.

Step 1 - Turn Windows Feature On

The first step is to go to the bottom left corner of your Start bar and click on the Windows icon, type "turn windows feature on/off" in the search bar. The option will be shown as seen below, click on it.

Step 2 - Enable Windows Subsystem For Linux

Scroll through the options until Windows Subsystem for Linux is shown. Check the box and click OK to save changes. A new window will pop up with choices to restart your computer now or later. You will need to restart your computer immediately before going any further.

Step 3 - Download Ubuntu

Upon restart of your computer, you could choose to download Ubuntu from Microsoft Store or from Ubuntu.


Any of the options above will have Ubuntu distribution downloaded and available on your computer.

Step 4 - Access Terminal Through Ubuntu or Command Prompt

- Ubuntu

Go to your Start bar and click on the Windows icon, look through your apps for Ubuntu or Bash on Ubuntu (as is my case, see image below).
Click to launch and the terminal will pop up. Ubuntu packages will be installed the first time the app is launched so it won't look exactly like the screenshot below but this is what it looks like upon completion.

- Command Prompt

Another option is to access the terminal from the Windows Command Prompt. Go to the bottom left corner of your Start bar and click on the Windows icon, type "command prompt" in the search bar. As soon as you click the option, the command prompt will pop up. Type bash and press enter to access terminal.

Congrats! You can now run shell commands on your computer without having an internet connection or relying on the sandbox provided on the intranet. Using the sandbox might be comfortable for the first few weeks however, it gets a bit uneasy as the projects come so take this advice from your big brother and install WSL on your computer.

There is another way to install Ubuntu (Vagrant) which you will encounter (if you haven't already) on the intranet however the scope of this article is limited to WSL. Please leave a comment in the comment box below if this article was helpful.

Items.Title: 下一个推特 | The Next Twitter
Items.Description:
世界对 Twitter 不满意。 许多非常糟糕的决定是由其傲慢的新领导层在匆忙和公开的情况下推动的。 这是一个难得的机会，可以见证糟糕的管理会对整个公司的团队和产品造成什么影响。

无论好坏，Twitter 一直是世界上最公开的扩音器。 在这里，我们作为一个集体去发泄、广播，并与各种各样的人进行有意义的对话，所有这些都是公开的。

网络从不缺乏良好的交流平台。 早在 Twitter 出现之前，大量的自托管平台帮助社区蓬勃发展、保持联系和分享知识。 论坛、Reddit，甚至邮件列表仍然被用作社区讨论的公共主机。

在我看来，Twitter 的独特之处在于双重：有限制，而且都是公开的。

人们想要的更少

如果今天有一种社会趋势，那就是在无限集合中呈现的简短内容。 人们不需要花时间阅读长篇文章或观看长视频。 他们可以很容易地浏览一段文字、图像或视频，然后决定它是否值得消费或跳到下一个。 这些限制不仅可以帮助我们判断内容是否值得关注，还可以让内容非常容易制作。

然后平台可以发展并有第一篇文章作为介绍更多。 推特上一个非常普遍的做法是在一系列回复的第一条推文末尾附加“一个话题🧵👇”，作者在其中吸引人们更深入地研究一个主题。 线程是推文限制的自然演变。 在 Instagram 上，您可以在一个帖子中添加多张照片和视频，而 TikTok 将视频长度延长至 10 分钟。 人们想要更多

该平台在推广简短内容的同时，还必须是庞大的，并能吸引用户有意义的参与感和成长潜力。

默认情况下，帖子是公开的、可搜索的并且对任何人的反应开放。 即使大多数用户内容没有被放大，它也有可能通过转发和回复来激励人们写作和参与更多。

这种传播特性也吸引了知名人士加入该平台并带来他们的内容和意见，为更多人参与提供了另一个理由。 大号可以免费接触到数百万人，老百姓有直接的沟通渠道。 但是等等，还有更多

虽然我确实相信 Twitter 的成功可以与这两个支柱相关联，但不可否认的是，许多其他因素对公司变得像今天这样重要也发挥了重要作用。

推特已经存在很长时间了。 它发展非常缓慢，做出了谨慎的产品决策[4] 。 它吸引了许多大玩家：艺术家、公司、政府。 它的网络和移动应用程序非常用户友好且响应迅速。 它的基础设施在处理大量生成的内容时变得非常可靠。 它变得如此主流，以至于借用了电子邮件中的“@”。

其中大部分是先有鸡还是先有蛋的情况。 您的产品可能需要数年时间才能真正在历史上找到自己的位置。 如果没有重要的用户，您就无法构建它并期望成功，而这些用户可能一开始就不存在。 当你不需要时，你不应该投资于基础设施。

从一开始，并希望在公司的整个生命周期中，人们可以做的就是投资于良好的产品决策和用户体验。 不用说，有证据表明 Twitter 完全放弃了前者。

对我来说， The Next Twitter包括以上所有内容。 它需要提供大量可以扩展的短格式内容、可以提高参与度并为平台带来更多用户的重要用户、在网络和移动设备上可能的最佳用户体验，以及良好的决策指导他们的用户和他们的愿景，而不是他们的胆量。

只有时间会给出答案。

社交媒体产品的许多方面都不是为我们的福祉量身定制的，而是为了确保我们参与和购买产品。


 许多新的较小的功能开始出现并围绕主时间线使产品膨胀。 Twitter has been completely musked today, and it is sad that product. Twitter 今天已经完全被屏蔽了，这个产品令人难过。



原文:

The world is not happy with Twitter. Lots of really bad decisions are being pushed by its arrogant new leadership, both in a hurry and in public. This it a unique opportunity to witness what bad management can do to teams and products on an entire company scale.

Twitter has been, for better or worse, the world’s most public megaphone. It is where we as a collective go vent, broadcast, and have meaningful conversations with all sorts of people, all in the open.

The web never lacked good platforms for communication. Long before Twitter a multitude of self hosted platforms helped communities flourish, keep in touch, and share knowledge. Forums, Reddit, heck even mailing lists are still used as public hosts of community discourse.

What made Twitter unique, in my perspective, was twofold: there was a limit, and it was all public.

People Want Less

If there is a societal trend today, it is short-form content presented in an infinite collection. People don’t need to invest their time reading a long article or watching a long video. They can easily glance at a piece of text, image or video and decide if it is worth consuming or skipping to the next one. [1]

Twitter limited us to 140 characters. Instagram photos have always been 1080 pixels wide. Vine videos couldn’t be longer than 6.5 seconds, and TikTok once limited them to 1 minute.

These limitations not only help us judge if the content deserves attention, it makes content very easy to produce.

Platforms can then evolve and have the first post as an introduction for more. A very common practice on Twitter is appending “A thread 🧵👇” at the end of the first tweet in a series of replies, where authors entice people to dive deeper into a subject. [2]

Threads were a natural evolution for the tweet limitation. On Instagram you can add multiple photos and videos in one post, and TikTok expanded video length up to 10 minutes long. Vine never did this, and it’s not around anymore.

People Want More

The platform, while still promoting brief content, must also be massive and entice the user’s sense of meaningful participation and potential to grow.

Twitter gives everyone an opportunity to write anything that can go viral. Posts are public, searchable and open to anyone’s reaction by default. Even if the majority of user content isn’t amplified, the potential that it can motivate people to write and engage more by retweeting and replying.

This broadcast characteristic also attracts famous people to join the platform and bring their content and opinions, giving yet another reason for more people to engage. Large accounts can reach millions of people for free, and the common folk gets a direct communication channel. [3]

But Wait, There’s More

While I do believe that Twitter’s success can be associated with those two pillars, it is undenyable that many other factors played significant roles for the company to become as relevant as it is today.

Twitter has been around for a very long time. It evolved very slowly, making careful product decisions[4]. It attracted many big players: artists, companies, governments. Its web and mobile apps are very user friendly and responsive. Its infrastructure got extremely reliable handling the immense amount of generated content. It became so mainstream it borrowed the “@“ from email to itself.

Most of these are chicken-and-egg scenarios. It might take years for your product to actually find its place in history. You can’t build it and expect success without important users, which might not be there at frist. You shouldn’t invest in infrastructure when you don’t need it.

What one can do from the start, and hopefully for the lifetime of the company, is invest in good product decisions and user experiences. Needless to say, evidence shows Twitter completely abandoned the former.

To me, The Next Twitter includes all of the above. It needs to provide a good amount of short-form content that can be expanded, important users that can raise engagement and bring more users to the platform, the best user experience possible on both the web and mobile devices, and good people making decisions guided by their users and their vision, not their guts.

It might be the case that Twitter itself collapses, ressurects and becomes its next self. Only time will tell.

Cover image generated by OpenAI.


[1] To be clear here: I don't believe this is a good practice, but an abuse of our limited attention. Many aspects of social media products are not tailored to our wellbeing, but to making sure we engage and buy products.


[2] I also want point out that this might be the key difference between TikTok and YouTube. TikTok presents the TV channel experience: content already plays, and you are able to skip it and therefore tune the algorithm to offer you better videos. YouTube presents you a catalog of thumbnails and titles, which you then have to choose from.


[3] This phenomenon also applies to brands, where customer service is often much better via Twitter than traditional ways, such as phone calls.


[4] I feel this started to shift by the time they introduced Fleets. Many new smaller features started to appear and bloat the product surrounding the main timeline. Twitter has been completely musked today, and it is sad that product.

Items.Title: 使用 Active Record 创建表的快速入门指南 | Quick Start guide to creating a table using Active Record
Items.Description:
对象关系映射 (ORM) 是一种允许您使用面向对象的方法查询和更改数据库中的数据的技术。 使用 ORM 时，您的类将是数据库表，这些类的实例将作为这些表中的行存在。 使用 ORM 允许开发人员减少重复代码，同时还可以实现常规的、有组织的模式。 这些做法使代码库保持清洁和可读性，这一点很重要，尤其是当许多开发人员将处理代码时。

在使用 ORM 时，您会看到一些值得注意的模式。 虽然类通常用单数词定义，但类的对应表将是复数。 如果我们有一个Book类，对应的表就是books 。 引入活动记录

要在您的代码中使用 Active Record gem，您只需在终端中运行gem install activerecord ，或者将其包含在代码的Gemfile中即可。

要使用 Active Record 的内置 ORM 方法，我们需要使我们的 Ruby 类成为ActiveRecord::Base的子类。

 class Book < ActiveRecord::Base end

现在我们的Book类可以与数据库中的books表对话。 使用 Active Record 时要记住的重要一点是 Active Record 遵循约定优于配置。 这意味着只要您遵循 Active Record 采用的约定，您就需要为您的模型编写很少甚至不需要的配置。 类名是单数而表名是复数是非常重要的。 只要开发人员遵循这条规则，Active Record 就可以为开发人员节省大量时间并使他们的代码更具可读性。

使我们的 Ruby 类成为 Active Record 的子类可以让它们访问许多方法。 一种非常有用的方法是attr_accessors可用于我们的代码，而无需显式写入它们。我在下面的 Active Record 中定义了一些其他有用的方法，但我建议任何对使用 Active Record 感兴趣的开发人员读取Active Record文档以查看它真正可以做的所有事情。

.column_names

检索表中列的列表：

 Book.column_names #=> [:id, :name, :author, :genre]
.create

为类创建一个新对象，并将实例保存到类对应的表中

Book.create(name: 'The Stranger', author: 'Albert Campus', genre: 'Philosophical novel') # INSERT INTO books (name, author, genre) VALUES ('The Stranger', 'Albert Campus', 'Philosophical Novel') #=> #<Book: 0x00009f785d0832b0 id: 1, name: "The Stranger", author: "Albert Campus", genre: "Philosophical novel">
.all

将表中的所有记录作为其类的实例返回

Book.all # SELECT "books".* FROM "books" # => [#<Book: 0x00009f785d0832b0 id: 1, name: "The Stranger", author: "Albert Campus", genre: "Philosophical novel">]
.find

通过id从数据库中检索对象：

 Book.find(1) # SELECT "books".* FROM "books" WHERE "books"."id" = 1 LIMIT 1 #=> #<Book: 0x00009f785d0832b0 id: 1, name: "The Stranger", author: "Albert Campus", genre: "Philosophical novel">
.find_by

通过任何属性查找对象，例如author

 Book.find_by(author: 'Albert Campus') # SELECT "books".* FROM "books" WHERE "books"."author" = 'Albert Campus' LIMIT 1 #=> #<Book: 0x00009f785d0832b0 id: 1, name: "The Stranger", author: "Albert Campus", genre: "Philosophical novel">

活动记录迁移

它们提供所做更改的历史记录，为我们的数据库提供版本控制。 使用 Active Record 迁移还允许我们放弃编写 SQL 语句来创建或更改表，因为 Active Record 会在后台为我们做这些。

为了告诉 Active Record 我们希望它如何连接到我们的数据库，我们将使用config/database.yml文件。 按照惯例，此文件与 Active Record 一起使用，以提供有关如何连接我们的数据库的详细信息。 对于这个例子，我们将使用 SQlite，但 Active Record 不支持其他数据库适配器。 这是它的样子：

 development: adapter: sqlite3 database: db/development.sqlite3 test: adapter: sqlite3 database: db/test.sqlite3

现在让我们设置我们的environment.rb文件。 这个文件需要我们的 Gemfile 中的 gem 来让我们的程序访问它们。 我们的environment.rb文件包含以下内容：

 ENV["RACK_ENV"] ||= "development" require 'bundler/setup' Bundler.require(:default, ENV["RACK_ENV"])

创建迁移

要创建一个迁移来设置我们的 books 表，我们将在我们的终端中运行以下命令：

 bundle exec rake db:create_migration NAME=create_books

我们使用 bundle exec rake 来访问我们的 rake 文件，该文件为我们提供了大量可以在终端中运行的命令，上面的代码用于创建新的迁移。 此命令在db/migrations中生成一个名为20221116095220_create_books.rb的新文件。 开始的时间戳对于 Active Record 至关重要，因为它告诉您的程序运行迁移的顺序。 以下代码将在新创建的文件中：

 class CreateBooks < ActiveRecord::Migration[6.1] def change end end

由于我们正在使用此迁移来创建新表，因此我们将使用create_table命令来执行此操作。 当我们创建我们的表时，我们将提供列的属性名称以及每个列的数据类型。 现在文件已经设置好并且您已经定义了表格的外观，我们必须运行迁移！ 这将根据您的迁移内容创建一个新的数据库文件，它还将创建一个db/schema.rb文件，让您快速查看数据库的设置。 我们在终端中使用以下命令运行我们的迁移：

 bundle exec rake db:migrate

在此阶段，我们的db/schema.rb文件将如下所示：

 ActiveRecord::Schema.define(version: 2022_11_16_095220) do create_table "books", force: :cascade do |t| t.string "name" t.string "author" t.string "genre" end end

而已！ 该表现在存在于数据库中，其中包含我们在迁移中定义的列以及一个 id 列。 这旨在作为开始使用 Active Record 迁移的简要指南，但是使用 Active Record 您可以做的还有很多！ 我鼓励您查看下面有关 Active Record 基础知识和 Active Record 协会的资源，以了解 Active Record 可以帮助您完成的所有工作。 快乐编码！

原文:

Object-Relational Mapping(ORM) is a technique that allows you to query and change data in a database using an object-oriented approach. When using an ORM, your classes will be database tables and instances of those classes will exist as rows in those tables. Using an ORM allows a developer to cut down on repetitive code while also implementing conventional, organized patterns. These practices keep a code base clean and readable, which is important especially when many developers will be working on the code.

There are patterns that you will see when using an ORM that are worth taking note of. While classes are typically defined with a singular word, a class's corresponding table will be plural. If we have a Book class, the corresponding table will be books. Instances of the Book class will be stored in the books table as rows, and attributes for those instances will be stored in columns in the books table.

Introducing Active Record

Active Record is an ORM library that can be used with Ruby. Active Record comes with many built in methods that make it easier to manipulate our data. To use the Active Record gem in your code, you can simply run gem install activerecord in your terminal, or by including it in your code's Gemfile.

To make use of Active Record's built-in ORM methods, we need to make our Ruby class a subclass of ActiveRecord::Base.

class Book < ActiveRecord::Base
end

Now our Book class is able to talk to the books table in the database. Something important to keep in mind when using Active Record is that Active Record follows convention over configuration. This means that as long as you follow the conventions adopted by Active Record, you will need to write very little to no configuration for your models. It is very important that your class names are singular and table names are plural. As long as a developer follows this rule, Active Record can save developer's a lot of time and make their code much more readable.

Some useful methods from Active Record

Making our Ruby classes subclasses of Active Record gives them access many methods. One very useful method is that attr_accessors are usable with our code without having to explicitly write them in. I've defined some other useful methods from Active Record below, but I recommend any developer that is interested in using Active Record to read the Active Record docs to see all that it can really do.

.column_names

Retrieve a list of the columns in a table:

Book.column_names
#=> [:id, :name, :author, :genre]
.create

Creates a new object for the class and saves the instance to the class's corresponding table

Book.create(name: 'The Stranger', author: 'Albert Campus', genre: 'Philosophical novel')
# INSERT INTO books (name, author, genre) VALUES ('The Stranger', 'Albert Campus', 'Philosophical Novel') 
#=> #<Book: 0x00009f785d0832b0 id: 1, name: "The Stranger", author: "Albert Campus", genre: "Philosophical novel">
.all

Return all the records from a table as instances of its class

Book.all
# SELECT "books".* FROM "books"
# => [#<Book: 0x00009f785d0832b0 id: 1, name: "The Stranger", author: "Albert Campus", genre: "Philosophical novel">]
.find

Retrieve an object from the database by its id:

Book.find(1)
# SELECT "books".* FROM "books" WHERE "books"."id" = 1 LIMIT 1
#=> #<Book: 0x00009f785d0832b0 id: 1, name: "The Stranger", author: "Albert Campus", genre: "Philosophical novel">
.find_by

Find an object by any attribute, such as author

Book.find_by(author: 'Albert Campus')
# SELECT "books".* FROM "books" WHERE "books"."author" = 'Albert Campus' LIMIT 1
#=> #<Book: 0x00009f785d0832b0 id: 1, name: "The Stranger", author: "Albert Campus", genre: "Philosophical novel">

Active Record Migrations

Active Record migrations are used to set up our database, and we use this format for many reasons. They provide a history of the changes made, providing version control for our database. Using Active Record migrations also allows us to forego writing SQL statements to create or change tables, as Active Record will do this for us under the hood.

To tell Active Record how we want it to connect to our database, we will use a config/database.yml file. This file is used by convention with Active Record to provide details about how to connect with our database. For this example, we will be using SQlite, but Active Record does support other database adaptors. This is what it looks like:

development:
  adapter: sqlite3
  database: db/development.sqlite3

test:
  adapter: sqlite3
  database: db/test.sqlite3

Now let's set up our environment.rb file. This file requires the gems in our Gemfile to give our program access them them. ENV["RACK_ENV"] is our environment variable, which is set to development in this example. RACK_ENV is a specific environment variable used by the sinatra-activerecord gem to determine what database to connect to. Our environment.rb file contains the following:

ENV["RACK_ENV"] ||= "development"

require 'bundler/setup'
Bundler.require(:default, ENV["RACK_ENV"])

Creating a migration

To create a migration to set up our books table, we will run the following command in our terminal:

bundle exec rake db:create_migration NAME=create_books

We use bundle exec rake to access our rake file that gives us a multitude of commands that can be run in the terminal, and the above code is used to create a new migration. This command generates a new file in db/migrations called 20221116095220_create_books.rb. The timestamp at the beginning is crucial for Active Record as it tells your program which order to run the migrations. The following code will be in the newly created file:

class CreateBooks < ActiveRecord::Migration[6.1]
  def change
  end
end

Since we are using this migration to create a new table, we will use the create_table command to do so. When we create our table, we will provide the attribute names for the columns as well as the datatype for each of those columns. The id column is dynamically generated, so there is no need to include it here!

class CreateBooks < ActiveRecord::Migration[6.1]
  def change
    create_table :books do |t|
      t.string :name
      t.string :genre
      t.string :author
    end
  end
end

Now that the file is set up and you've defined how you want your table to look, we must run the migration! This will create a new database file based on the contents of your migration, and it will also create a db/schema.rb file which will give you a quick view of the set up of your database. We run our migration with the following command in the terminal:

bundle exec rake db:migrate

At this stage, our db/schema.rb file will look like this:

ActiveRecord::Schema.define(version: 2022_11_16_095220) do
  create_table "books", force: :cascade do |t|
    t.string "name"
    t.string "author"
    t.string "genre"
  end
end

That's it! The table now exists in the database with the columns that we have defined in our migration, as well as an id column. This is meant to serve as a brief guide to get started with Active Record migrations, but there is so much more that you can do using Active Record! Some of the most powerful use cases for Active Record involve creating associations between classes and tables. I encourage you to check out the resources below on Active Record Basics and Active Record associations to see all that Active Record can help you accomplish. Happy Coding!

Donc ici fake-kubelet est une simulation d'un nœud de Kubernetes. Il ya un fake-k8s ici, qui peut démarrer un cluster en utilisant les nœuds de simulation de fake-kubelet. Il peut être utilisé comme une alternative à Kind dans certains scénarios où vous n'avez pas besoin d'exécuter réellement le Pod …

Considérant les limites de Kubernetes, un outil de simulation peut être utile même s'il ne permettra pas réellement de tester les comportements et consommation de ressources réels surtout dans le cas de grands clusters :

Considerations for large clusters

Pour rappel =>

Un cluster est un ensemble de nœuds (machines physiques ou virtuelles) exécutant des agents Kubernetes, gérés par le plan de contrôle. Kubernetes v1.25 prend en charge des clusters comptant jusqu'à 5000 nœuds. Plus précisément, Kubernetes est conçu pour s'adapter aux configurations qui répondent à tous les critères suivants :

  • Pas plus de 110 Pods par nœud
  • Pas plus de 5000 Nœuds
  • Pas plus de 150000 Pods au total
  • Pas plus de 300 000 Conteneurs au total

Vous pouvez faire évoluer votre cluster en ajoutant ou en supprimant des nœuds. La manière dont vous procédez dépend de la façon dont votre cluster est déployé …

Donc ici fake-kubelet est une simulation d’un nœud de Kubernetes. Il y a un fake-k8s ici, qui peut démarrer un cluster en utilisant les nœuds de simulation de fake-kubelet. Il peut être utilisé comme une alternative à Kind dans certains scénarios où vous n’avez pas besoin d’exécuter réellement le Pod …

Considérant les limites de Kubernetes, un outil de simulation peut être utile même s’il ne permettra pas réellement de tester les comportements et consommation de ressources réels surtout dans le cas de grands clusters :

Considerations for large clusters

Pour rappel =>

Un cluster est un ensemble de nœuds (machines physiques ou virtuelles) exécutant des agents Kubernetes, gérés par le plan de contrôle. Kubernetes v1.25 prend en charge des clusters comptant jusqu’à 5000 nœuds. Plus précisément, Kubernetes est conçu pour s’adapter aux configurations qui répondent à tous les critères suivants :

  • Pas plus de 110 Pods par nœud
  • Pas plus de 5000 Nœuds
  • Pas plus de 150000 Pods au total
  • Pas plus de 300 000 Conteneurs au total

Vous pouvez faire évoluer votre cluster en ajoutant ou en supprimant des nœuds. La manière dont vous procédez dépend de la façon dont votre cluster est déployé …

À suivre !
